brief.projectTitle: Marvel Cinematic Universe: Movie Launch Experience
brief.projectDescription: A high-impact, cinematic movie promotion platform designed to drive ticket sales and fan engagement through immersive storytelling and character exploration.

productScope.mvpDefinition: A high-fidelity landing page featuring a cinematic hero section, interactive character roster, media gallery, and a direct ticket booking integration.
productScope.coreOutcome: Convert visitors into ticket buyers and social media advocates by delivering a 'superhero' digital experience.
productScope.businessGoal: Maximize opening weekend box office revenue and build franchise hype.
productScope.audienceSummary: Marvel fans, movie enthusiasts, and casual cinema-goers aged 13-45.
inScope: Ticket booking CTA and theater locator

roles.summary: A user looking for movie details, trailers, and ticket information.
permissions: Public access to all promotional content

taskLoops.name: Ticket Booking Journey
taskLoops.summary: The path from landing on the page to selecting a theater for tickets.
taskLoops.entryTrigger: User lands on the page or clicks a 'Book Now' ad.
taskLoops.successState: User is redirected to the checkout page of a ticketing partner.

capabilities.name: Immersive Content Delivery
capabilities.summary: High-performance video and parallax image delivery to create a 'Marvel' feel.

capabilities.name: Ticketing Integration
capabilities.summary: Seamless transition from promotional content to third-party ticket vendors.

designIntent.colorDirection: Deep 'Marvel Red' (#ED1D24), metallic silver accents, and dark slate/black backgrounds for high contrast.
designIntent.style: Modern comic-book aesthetic with heavy sans-serif typography (e.g., Impact or custom Marvel fonts), parallax scrolling, and glowing neon accents.

informationArchitecture.navigationModel: Single-page immersive scroll with secondary deep-dive pages for characters.

pageMap.title: Main Feature
pageMap.purpose: Capture attention with the trailer and provide a high-level overview of the movie.

pageMap.title: The Heroes & Villains
pageMap.purpose: Detailed background on the movie's cast and characters.

sharedShells: Global Sticky Navigation with 'Buy Tickets' CTA
notes: Navigation should be semi-transparent to allow visuals to bleed through.
Mobile experience must prioritize the 'Get Tickets' button.

layoutSections.intent: Provide quick access to trailer, characters, and tickets.
layoutSections.contentHints: Marvel-style logo, links to sections, prominent red 'GET TICKETS' button.

designPlan.goal: Maintain brand presence while providing a persistent path to conversion.
designPlan.roleFit: Optimized for the Movie Fan who needs immediate access to tickets at any scroll depth.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: Supports the 'Ticket Booking Journey' by keeping the final step visible.
designPlan.capabilityFocus: Exposes the Ticketing Integration via a high-contrast CTA.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Logo left, primary links center, 'GET TICKETS' button right.
designPlan.layoutIntent: Sticky, semi-transparent glassmorphism to blend with cinematic backgrounds.
designPlan.visualIntent: Bold typography and Marvel Red accents for the primary CTA.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Smooth hover states on links; button should have a subtle glow or pulse.
designPlan.contentStrategy: Minimalist navigation to keep focus on the movie visuals.
constraints: Must remain legible over dark and light video backgrounds.
Mobile view must collapse to a hamburger menu but keep the CTA visible.
designPlan.rationale: A sticky, high-contrast nav ensures the primary business goal (ticket sales) is never more than a click away.

layoutSections.contentHints: Marvel Studios logo, social media icons, copyright info, privacy policy.

designPlan.goal: Provide necessary legal info and social proof while grounding the page in the Marvel brand.
designPlan.roleFit: Serves fans looking for social media connections and legal/press info.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: End-of-page catch-all for users who have finished the main scroll.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Logo, social grid, legal links, and partner logos.
designPlan.layoutIntent: Structured grid with a dark, slate-colored background.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Standard link behaviors with subtle icon hover effects.
designPlan.contentStrategy: Prioritize social media icons to build community hype.
constraints: Must include standard Marvel legal disclaimers.
Social icons should be high-fidelity.
designPlan.rationale: The footer provides a professional close to the high-energy experience, ensuring brand consistency.

pages.title: Movie Home
pages.description: The main landing experience for the movie promotion.

sections.intent: Immediate visual impact with the main trailer.
sections.contentHints: Auto-playing muted background video, large movie title logo, 'Watch Trailer' play button.

designPlan.role: Page opener and value proposition anchor
designPlan.goal: Capture 100% of the user's attention with high-fidelity video.
designPlan.roleFit: Immediately satisfies the fan's desire for 'blockbuster' visuals.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: The entry point of the Ticket Booking Journey.
designPlan.capabilityFocus: Demonstrates 'Immersive Content Delivery' through full-screen video.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Centered movie logo, secondary 'Watch Trailer' button, and scroll indicator.
designPlan.visualIntent: Dark, cinematic, and high-contrast with the Marvel Red logo.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Play button opens a high-res trailer in a modal.
designPlan.contentStrategy: Visuals first; minimal text to avoid cluttering the cinematic frame.
constraints: Video must be muted and loop seamlessly.
Logo must be high-resolution SVG.
designPlan.rationale: The hero section sets the tone and establishes the 'superhero' scale of the experience.

sections.contentHints: Dramatic summary text, character cutout images, 'Only in Theaters' release date.

designPlan.goal: Explain why the movie matters and build emotional stakes.
designPlan.roleFit: Engages fans who want to know the 'what' and 'why' of the story.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: Builds interest to fuel the eventual ticket purchase.
designPlan.capabilityFocus: Uses parallax cutouts to enhance storytelling.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Large headline, dramatic body copy, and floating character assets.
designPlan.layoutIntent: Split layout with text on one side and a dynamic character cutout on the other.
designPlan.visualIntent: Gritty textures and bold, heavy typography.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Subtle parallax movement as the user scrolls.
designPlan.contentStrategy: Short, punchy sentences that sound like a movie trailer script.
constraints: Text must be highly legible against dark backgrounds.
Character cutouts must have clean alpha channels.
designPlan.rationale: The synopsis bridges the gap between 'cool visuals' and 'compelling story'.

sections.contentHints: Interactive cards that expand on hover, featuring hero names and actors.

designPlan.goal: Allow users to explore the cast and build personal connections to characters.
designPlan.roleFit: Directly serves the 'Learn about new characters' goal for fans.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: Deepens engagement during the evaluation stage.
designPlan.capabilityFocus: Interactive UI elements that feel 'high-tech' or 'Stark-like'.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Grid of character portraits with names and actor credits.
designPlan.layoutIntent: Dynamic grid with varying card sizes or a horizontal slider.
designPlan.visualIntent: Glowing borders, metallic textures, and vibrant character colors.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Hovering on a card reveals a 'Dossier' link and character stats.
designPlan.contentStrategy: Focus on character names and iconic imagery.
constraints: Cards must be touch-friendly for mobile users.
Hover effects should not cause layout shifts.
designPlan.rationale: The character roster is a fan-favorite feature that encourages exploration and sharing.

sections.contentHints: Horizontal scroll of high-res stills and behind-the-scenes clips.

designPlan.goal: Showcase the scale and quality of the film's production.
designPlan.roleFit: Satisfies the 'Watch trailers/stills' goal for the visitor.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: Final visual push before the ticketing section.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Masonry or horizontal scroll gallery with lightbox functionality.
designPlan.visualIntent: Clean, dark UI that lets the colorful movie stills pop.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Clicking an image opens a full-screen high-res lightbox.
designPlan.contentStrategy: Curate the most action-packed and visually stunning frames.
constraints: Images must be lazy-loaded for performance.
Lightbox must support swipe gestures on mobile.
designPlan.rationale: A high-quality gallery provides the 'proof' that the movie is a must-see event.

sections.contentHints: Zip code search, theater list, time selection buttons.

designPlan.goal: Make finding showtimes and buying tickets as frictionless as possible.
designPlan.roleFit: Directly serves the 'Find showtimes and buy tickets' goal.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: The climax and success state of the Ticket Booking Journey.
designPlan.capabilityFocus: Ticketing Integration via third-party API or redirect.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Location input, date selector, theater list, and time buttons.
designPlan.layoutIntent: Clean, functional card-based layout with clear hierarchy.
designPlan.visualIntent: High-contrast 'Marvel Red' buttons and clear, bold labels.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Real-time filtering of theaters based on zip code.
constraints: Must handle 'no theaters found' states gracefully.
Buttons must be large enough for easy tapping.
designPlan.rationale: This section is the primary business driver and must be the most functional part of the site.

pageDesignPlan.pageGoal: The main landing experience for the movie promotion.
pageDesignPlan.roleFit: This page primarily serves Movie Fan.
pageDesignPlan.capabilityFocus: This page should make Immersive Content Delivery, Ticketing Integration understandable and actionable.
pageDesignPlan.taskLoopCoverage: Help users progress through Ticket Booking Journey, Ticket Booking Journey.
pageDesignPlan.narrativeArc: Move from orientation to persuasion, then social proof, and finish with a decisive conversion close.
pageDesignPlan.layoutStrategy: Use section rhythm, spacing, and contrast to support the entry stage of the user journey rather than making every block equally loud.
hierarchy: The first viewport should establish context and value quickly.
Mid-page sections should deepen trust or explain the offer.
Lower-page sections should reduce friction and reinforce action.
pageDesignPlan.transitionStrategy: Make transitions feel intentional through spacing, contrast shifts, and restrained motion rather than decorative separators.
sharedShellNotes: Assume global layout sections frame the page experience.
Keep page-specific sections visually coherent with the shared shell.
constraints: Preserve the given section order unless a wrapper or grouping is required for coherence.
Avoid repetitive section pacing; vary density and emphasis across the page.
pageDesignPlan.rationale: Default page planner framed Movie Home around role needs and the MVP journey rather than isolated sections.

pages.title: Character Dossier
pages.description: Deep dive into individual character lore.

sections.intent: Introduce the character with high-impact art.
sections.contentHints: Full-height character portrait, power stats, origin summary.

designPlan.goal: Present a character in a way that feels like a high-end digital collectible.
designPlan.roleFit: Satisfies the fan's desire for detailed character information.
designPlan.taskLoopFocus: Deepens the user's connection to the film's cast.
designPlan.capabilityFocus: Uses 'Immersive Content Delivery' for high-res character renders.
designPlan.informationArchitecture: Character name, actor name, power level bars, and biography text.
designPlan.layoutIntent: Editorial-style layout with a large portrait and overlayed text.
designPlan.visualIntent: Bold, heroic, and stylized with character-specific color accents.
designPlan.interactionIntent: Animated power bars that fill up on load.
designPlan.contentStrategy: Use 'in-universe' language to describe powers and origins.
constraints: Portrait must be high-resolution and optimized for mobile.
Stats must be easy to read at a glance.
designPlan.rationale: The dossier header turns a simple bio into a cinematic experience.

pageDesignPlan.pageGoal: Deep dive into individual character lore.
pageDesignPlan.roleFit: This page primarily serves Movie Fan.
pageDesignPlan.capabilityFocus: This page should make Immersive Content Delivery understandable and actionable.
pageDesignPlan.taskLoopCoverage: Help users progress through the core user journey.
pageDesignPlan.narrativeArc: Keep the page tightly focused: establish value, support it, then convert.
pageDesignPlan.layoutStrategy: Use section rhythm, spacing, and contrast to support the evaluation stage of the user journey rather than making every block equally loud.
hierarchy: The first viewport should establish context and value quickly.
Mid-page sections should deepen trust or explain the offer.
Lower-page sections should reduce friction and reinforce action.
pageDesignPlan.transitionStrategy: Make transitions feel intentional through spacing, contrast shifts, and restrained motion rather than decorative separators.
sharedShellNotes: Assume global layout sections frame the page experience.
Keep page-specific sections visually coherent with the shared shell.
constraints: Preserve the given section order unless a wrapper or grouping is required for coherence.
Avoid repetitive section pacing; vary density and emphasis across the page.
pageDesignPlan.rationale: Default page planner framed Character Dossier around role needs and the MVP journey rather than isolated sections.
